message:
feat: add CJK bigram tokenization for Chinese/Japanese/Korean search

The RegexTokenizer pattern only matched ASCII alphanumerics, silently
dropping CJK characters during indexing. Chinese, Japanese, and Korean
queries returned zero results.

Fix: Expanded the regex to capture contiguous CJK character runs, added
a CjkBigramDecompose token filter that decomposes CJK tokens into
overlapping character bigrams (e.g. "搜索引擎" → ["搜索","索引","引擎"]),
and added CJK-aware query building that generates AND-of-bigrams queries.

Covers CJK Unified Ideographs, Extension A/B, Hiragana, Katakana,
Hangul, Bopomofo, and CJK Compatibility ranges. Schema hash bumped to
trigger automatic full rebuild on upgrade.

Includes 12 new tests: CJK detection, bigram decomposition, and
end-to-end index-and-search roundtrips for Chinese, Japanese, and Korean.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
